# UC-10.12.57 · Collaboration Platform Session Analytics (Zoom/Teams Quality)

## Description

Rolls meeting-quality telemetry—including average packet loss, jitter, and round-trip latency—by remote worker identity and inferred location tag to pinpoint chronic poor sessions rather than blame one-off outages.

## Value

Workplace productivity and employee experience sponsors can prioritize network remediation, ISP stipends, or office hoteling where collaboration tools consistently degrade, sustaining engagement for distributed recruiting and retention goals.

## Implementation

Land Zoom quality metrics dashboards through supported modular inputs polling Admin API aggregates. Combine Microsoft Teams call records via Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services ensuring personal email aliases match HR directories. Sample daily baselines to avoid reacting to single meetings with bad Wi-Fi.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• OAuth consent for Zoom and Microsoft Graph scopes covering call quality; secure secret storage on modular input hosts.
• Directory join between `user_email` and HR location fields for meaningful `user_location`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Throttle API polling responsibly (for example hourly rollups per user). Redact recording URLs or meeting titles at ingest unless legally approved. Harmonize percent versus fractional packet loss notation using `eval` normalization.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Tune thresholds alongside your WAN team:

```spl
index=collab sourcetype IN ("zoom:meeting_quality","teams:call_quality")
| stats avg(packet_loss_pct) as avg_loss avg(jitter_ms) as avg_jitter avg(latency_ms) as avg_latency count as meetings by user_email user_location
| where avg_loss > 2 OR avg_jitter > 30 OR avg_latency > 200
| sort - avg_loss
```

Understanding this SPL

**Collaboration Platform Session Analytics (Zoom/Teams Quality)** summarizes repeated softphone metrics per employee anchor so chronic sufferers surface before help-desk tickets pile up.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• `stats` collapses many meetings per person into rolling averages over the selected window.
• OR logic across loss, jitter, or latency captures different failure modes.
• `sort - avg_loss` prioritizes the most audible defects first.

Step 3 — Validate
Cross-check a subset with vendor-native dashboards (Zoom Dashboard, Teams Call Quality Dashboard). Confirm meeting counts align with calendar participation to avoid mis-attribution from shared devices.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Offer self-service Wi-Fi tips before formal IT spend. Respect privacy expectations by aggregating HR-only views separately from managerial rollups; never auto-discipline using media metrics alone. Consider visualizations listed above plus anomaly overlays after four weeks baselining.

## SPL

```spl
index=collab sourcetype IN ("zoom:meeting_quality","teams:call_quality")
| stats avg(packet_loss_pct) as avg_loss avg(jitter_ms) as avg_jitter avg(latency_ms) as avg_latency count as meetings by user_email user_location
| where avg_loss > 2 OR avg_jitter > 30 OR avg_latency > 200
| sort - avg_loss
```

## Visualization

Scatter plot (latency versus packet loss), Table (user_email, location, averages), Choropleth map by user_location aggregates.

## Known False Positives

Short calls on cellular hotspots, parental leave returnees testing docking stations, intentional audio-off video-only broadcasts, vendor brownouts confined to narrow regions—all can trip thresholds without enduring productivity loss.
